eerie: adjective. Inspiring fear, especially in a mysterious or shadowy way; strange, weird.
engine: noun. A large construction used in warfare, such as a battering ram, catapult etc.
engineer: noun. A soldier engaged in designing or constructing military works for attack or defence, or other engineering works.
erg: noun. A unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
genie: noun. A jinn, a being descended from the jann, normally invisible to the human eye, but who may also appear in animal or human form.
genre: noun. A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
ginner: noun. A person who operates a cotton gin.
gree: noun. One of a flight of steps.
green: noun. The color of grass and leaves; a primary additive color midway between yellow and blue which is evoked by light between roughly
greenie: noun. An environmentalist, someone who shows concern for the environment; often aimed at environmental extremists.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inner: adjective. Being or occurring (farther) inside, situated farther in, located (situated) or happening on the inside of something, situated
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
nene: noun. The Hawaiian goose, Branta sandvicensis, which was designated the state bird of Hawaii in 1957.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reign: noun. The exercise of sovereign power.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
renege: verb. To break a promise or commitment; to go back on one's word. [with on]
renin: noun. A circulating enzyme released by mammalian kidneys that converts angiotensinogen to angiotensin I.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
erning: verb. present participle and gerund of ern
genin: noun. The steroid -related portion of some types of glycosides
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
irene: noun. The hydrocarbon formed by dehydration of an irone.
neger: noun. Synonym of negro or nigger.
nerine: noun. Any of various plants of the genus Nerine, native to South Africa, used ornamentally, and toxic to many mammals.
niger: noun. An Ethiopian herb, Guizotia abyssinica, grown for its seed and edible oil.
niner: noun. Something nine inches long, or holding nine gallons, etc. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
ringe: noun. A whisk made of heather.
